finding: A lane adoption event was written under `.atm/history/session-events/...` as an untracked file, classified as manual-review residue, and then treated by `atm next` as unowned dirty WIP requiring an L3 Broker even though there were zero active claims, actors, locks, or staged files.
expectedBehavior: Ephemeral lane/session events should either be written under ignored runtime storage or be lifecycle-managed into a compact tracked digest; they should not leave ambiguous untracked history files that block or distract later governed work.
evidenceOrRepro: After ATM-GOV-0195 closeback, `git status --short` showed `?? .atm/history/session-events/lane-20260719134847-codex-governance-optimizer-ef8a433602/`. A later `node atm.mjs next --prompt ... --json` reported activeClaimCount=0 and activeActors=[], yet set hasForeignActiveWork=true and recommended Team L3 solely because that same ATM-managed file was unowned dirty WIP.
followUp: Decide whether lane session events are runtime logs or tracked evidence. If runtime, move them to `.atm/runtime/session-events` and gitignore them; if evidence, add deterministic seal/cleanup and pre-commit recognition.
